title: Threat model
description: E108-S3 / AF-2026-06-03-1 / Test17 D-2/D-6. Backing JSON Schema for the `threat-model` artifact type so /gaia-val-validate can enforce structural invariants, not merely route the type. The threat-model artifact is a prose-markdown document (STRIDE threat identification + DREAD risk scoring) with a YAML frontmatter metadata block (grounded in the on-disk corpus instance `.gaia/artifacts/planning-artifacts/threat-model.md` plus the /gaia-threat-model SKILL Step structure). The shared validate-artifact-schema.sh helper (E108-S5) extracts the frontmatter for *.md instances and validates it against THIS schema; the prose body is not machine-validated by a frontmatter schema, so the nine canonical H2 sections it MUST carry are documented in `x-required-sections` below for the document-ruleset and reviewer. Note: `threat-model` is ALREADY present in the /gaia-val-validate artifact_type enum (position 5) — E108-S3 adds the backing schema only, with NO enum change. Pragmatic, grounded shape — required metadata is pinned, but additional frontmatter keys (e.g. `framework_version`, `methodology`, `author`) are permitted.

template.description: Template identifier. MUST be `threat-model` — the slug that selects the document-specific ruleset and aligns with the on-disk artifact filename per the /gaia-val-validate artifact_type enum.

version.description: Template/document version (semver-shaped, e.g. `1.0.0`). Bumped when the threat model is re-run under a newer template. Note: quote in YAML frontmatter so a value like `1.0` is not parsed as a float by the validate-artifact-schema.sh YAML->JSON bridge.

date.description: Threat-model date. ISO-8601 `YYYY-MM-DD`. Quote in YAML frontmatter (`date: "2026-03-14"`) so the validate-artifact-schema.sh YAML->JSON bridge does not load it as a non-serializable date object (E108-S5 F-1).

project.description: Human-readable project name the threat model covers (e.g. `GAIA Framework`).

methodology.description: Optional. The threat-modelling methodology, when recorded (e.g. `STRIDE + DREAD`). Informational provenance, present in the corpus instance header.

author.description: Optional. The authoring persona/agent, when recorded (e.g. `Zara (Application Security Expert)`). Informational provenance.

framework_version.description: Optional. The GAIA framework version at threat-model time, when recorded. Informational provenance.

x-required-sections.description: The nine canonical H2 (`##`) sections the prose body MUST carry, grounded in the corpus instance + /gaia-threat-model Step structure. In the corpus instance these H2 headings are numbered (e.g. `## 1. Assets Inventory`); the section list below is the unnumbered canonical name. Validated by the document-ruleset / reviewer, not by this frontmatter schema.
